typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (no mypy/pyright in project)"

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: MCP tools should have at least a basic unit test covering function signature and edge cases (empty DB, invalid input). Currently no MCP tool has dedicated tests, creating a growing coverage gap.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for mcp-resume-context and mcp-feedback-tool both note no unit tests added. Grep for '@mcp.tool' in test files returns zero matches across 26 tests. 6 MCP tools now exist without any dedicated test coverage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: Use try/finally or context manager pattern for DB connections (conn = get_conn(); try: ... finally: conn.close()). Pre-existing pattern of bare conn.close() without exception protection risks connection leaks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp_server.py lines 54-62 (memory_search), 119-121 (memory_semantic_search), 571-610 (memory_feedback) all use conn = get_conn() followed by conn.close() without try/finally. Pattern is consistent but fragile.

rejectedObservations.observation: No SKILL.md file found for the 'python-worker' skill at .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md.
rejectedObservations.reason: Out of scope for this milestone. The python-worker skill is a project droid, not a file managed by workers.

rejectedObservations.observation: Error/solution categories have zero facts in DB despite being defined in FACT_CATEGORIES.
rejectedObservations.reason: Expected behavior — categories were just added and no real distill runs have occurred with the new prompt to categorize facts as error/solution.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker followed all AGENTS.md conventions correctly (from cross-project-patterns review).
Worker followed the python-worker skill procedure correctly (from cross-project-patterns review).

nonBlockingIssues.description: When session_id is explicitly provided but doesn't exist, returns nearly empty response instead of 'session not found' message.
Redundant SELECT DISTINCT with GROUP BY session_id.
In --promote path, p['pattern'] uses direct key lookup which could raise KeyError if LLM returns malformed dict. Display path safely uses p.get() but promote INSERT does not.
Corrected fact inserted via store_fact() has NULL timestamp, so it won't appear in time-filtered queries.
Option 1 documents a MEMORY_DB_PATH env var that doesn't yet exist in the codebase. While transparent about it, readers could initially mistake this for existing functionality.
